Tests if converting negative zero to a string results in "0".

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of the `String` function when it is applied to the numeric value `-0`. The test verifies that converting `-0` to a string results in the string `"0"`. This is a narrow functionality test, focusing on a specific edge case in JavaScript's type conversion behavior. The use of `assert` indicates that the code is intended to validate this specific behavior, ensuring that the conversion meets expected standards.
